name: Coahuila (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Coahuila centred.
history: Coahuila has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.
The arms feature emblems of the agricultural, mining, ranching and industrial traditions of Coahuila, with references to the river Rio Grande forming the northern border with the United States.
